div.p:
RELIGION, THE ARTS, AND THE SCIENCES
Humanities 127a
Examination
Theology 185a
Sat., Jan. 30, 1960
Prof. Paul Tillich
9:15 a.m.
(The following two questions will contribute equally to the examination
grade. Your ability to state the answers sharply and succinctly will
also be considered in the grading. Hence, please think before you write.
Total writing time should not be over 2 1/2 hours.)
I. What is the import of the topic "language" for discussing the
relation of religion and culture? Your answer should indicate
your grasp of lecture and assigned reading material, and your
own creative evaluation of the material.
II. Identify and discuss the significance of the following phrases
for relating religion and culture:
a. "Religion … is to have life and know life in immediate feeling."
b. "I – thou."
c. "Double symbolization."
d. "the Christ of Culture."

FINAL EXAMINATION
Hum. 127b – Theol. 185b
Prof. Tillich
Friday, June 3, 2:15 p.m.
Write a two hour essay on the following topic (outline form
may be used) :
Discuss the import of the readings and lectures of this course
for your own personal structuring of the ethical encounter.
It is strongly advised that the student spend considerable time
thinking and organizing his answer before writing. Your answer
will not be graded as to its quantity, but rather as to its
ability to integrate creatively the reading and lecture material
of this course in a personal statement of your understanding
of ethics.
If this question seems too easy, it isn't.

Humanities 127a
Theology 185a
Professor Paul Tillich
Tues. and Thurs., 11 – 12, (Sat.)
The required work for this semester course will consist of:
1. A minimum of 1300 pages of outside reading. The starred selections are
required and will be covered on the final examination.
2. A final examination. On this exam there will be included a one hour question
asking the student to write an essay on some topic of his own interest upon
which he has done research during the semester and for which he will present
a prepared bibliography along with the examination.
BLIOGRAPHY
General material
Buber, Martin
The Eclipse of God
Brunner, Emil
Christianity and Civilization
Eliot, T. S.
Notes on the Definition of Culture
Frank, Erich
*Philosophical Understanding and Religious Truth
Jaspers, Karl
Man in the Modern Age
Kroner, Richard
Culture and Faith
Niebuhr, H. Richard
*Christ and Culture
The Meaning of Revelation
Otto, Rudolf
The Idea of the Holy
Randall, John H.
The Making of the Modern Mind
Schleiermacher, F.
On Religion
Tillich, Paul
The Protestant Era
The Religious Situation
Religion and Language
Buber, Martin
Between Man and Man
Bultmann, Rudolf
*Kerygma and Myth (The opening essay by Bultmann)
Cassirer, Ernst
Language and Myth
Flew, A. and MacIntyre
*New Essays in Philosophical Theology (Ch. I, VI, VII)
Tillich, Paul
Dynamics of Faith
Urban, Wilbur M.
Language and Reality
Religion and Science
Burtt, E. A.
The Metaphysical Foundations of Modern Science
Fromm, E.
Psychoanalysis and Religion
Heim, Karl
*Christian Faith and Natural Science
Outler, Albert
Psychotherapy and the Christian Message
Roberts, David E.
*Psychotherapy and the Christian View of Man
Sartre, Jean Paul
Existential Psychoanalysis
Stern, Karl
The Third Revolution
Tillich, Paul
The Courage to Be
Love, Power, and Justice
Religion and the Arts
Fergusson, Francis
The Idea of a Theatre
Goddard, Harold
The Meaning of Shakespeare
Maritain, Jacques
Creative Intuition in Art and Poetry
Michalson, Carl
Christianity and the Existentialists
Ortega y Gasset
*The Dehumanization of Art

Religion and Culture
Humanities 127
Theology 131
Book List
Kroner, R.
CULTURE AND FAITH
Niebuhr, H. R.
CHRIST AND CULTURE
Tillich, P.
SYSTEMATIC THEOLOGY I
THE PROTESTANT ERA
Schleiermacher, F.
SPEECHES ON RELIGION
D'Arcy
THE NATURE OF BELIEF
Kroner, R.
THE PRIMACY OF FAITH
Ogden, C.
THE MEANING OF MEANING
(on language)
Urban, W.
LANGUAGE AND REALITY
Cassirer, E.
LANGUAGE AND MYTH
Randall, T.
THE MAKING OF THE MODERN MIND
Cassirer, E.
THE PROBLEM OF KNOWLEDGE
Wild, T.
THE CHALLENGE OF EXISTENTIALISM
Marcel, G.
THE PHILOSOPHY OF EXISTENCE, BEING AND HAVING
Heidegger, M.
EXISTENCE AND BEING
Kuhn, H.
ENCOUNTER WITH NOTHINGNESS
Cohen, M.
REASON AND NATURE
Oppenheimer, T. R.
SCIENCE AND THE COMMON UNDERSTANDING
Bailley, T.
SCIENCE AND FAITH TODAY
Raven, C.
SCIENCE AND THE CHRISTIAN MAN
Richardson, A.
SCIENCE, HISTORY AND FAITH
Heim, K.
CHRISTIAN FAITH AND NATURAL SCIENC
Lindsay, A. D.
RELIGION, SCIENCE AND SOCIETY IN THE MODERN WORID
Toynbee, A.
CIVILZATION ON TRIAL
Tillich, P.
THE INTERPRETATION OF HISTORY
Niebuhr, R.
BEYOND TRAGEDY – FAITH AND HISTORY
Heimann, E.
FREEDOM AND ORDER
Bennet, T.
CHRISTIANITY AND COMMUNISM
Niebuhr, R.
CHRISTIANITY AND POWER POLITICS
Fromm, E.
PSYCHOANALYSIS AND RELIGION
THE FEAR OF FREEDOM
MAN FOR HIMSELF
Roberts, R.
PSYCHOTHERAPY AND A CHRISTIAN VIEW OF MAN
Freud, S.
BASIC WRITINGS
Thomas, G.
RELIGION IN AN AGE OF SECULARISM
Ramsey, P.
CHRISTIAN ETHICS
Buber, M.
I AND THOU
Tillich, P.
LOVE, POWER AND JUSTICE
Ortega Y Gasset
THE REVOLT OF THE MASSES
Brunner, E.
JUSTICE AND SOCIAL ORDER
Ellul, T.
THE THEOLOGICAL FOUNDATION OF JUSTICE (in French)
Heimann, E.
FREEDOM AND ORDER

FINAL EXAM
Theology 185a
Professor Tillich
Humanities 127a
1. Write the essay you have prepared on some topic of your own choosing.
(Be sure to hand in the essay bibliography with your exam.) (1½ hrs.)
Answer three (3) of the following four (4) questions. (½ hr.. each)
(Avoid giving an answer that would duplicate the material covered in
your essay question above.)
2. Discuss the history of the struggle between science and theology, and
appraise their relationship at the present time. Give special attention
on the present scene to the thought of Tillich and Roberts (Psychotherapy
and the Christian View of Man).
3. Compare and evaluate the phrases 'religion as ultimate concern' and
'religion as the worship of God.' In answering, give reference to the
chapter on "The Existence of God" in Frank's Philosophical Understanding
and Religious Truth.
4. Discuss the relevance of Bultmann's essay on 'demythologizing' and
McPherson's essay on "Religion as the Inexpressible" (in New Essays in
Philosophical Theology) for Tillich's analysis of religious language.
5. Evaluate Tillich's method of appraising art. In answering, give reference
to particular artistic creations (in any realm – visual, poetry, drama,
etc.).

RELIGION AND CULTURE Semester II
1. The special character of art between theory and practice.
2. The doctrine of artistic symbols. (Reflective and representative symbols
psychological symbols, symbols in the different arts – music, architecture)
3. Theory of the 4 levels in the different arts.
(The problem of subject matter in music)
4. Examples of the 4 levels in the visual arts. (Slides)
5. Discussion and beginning analysis of the present period in the light of the arts.
*Malraux, A. THE TWILIGHT OF THE ABSOLUTE
PSYCHOLOGY OF ART (1950)
*Maritain, J. and
Cocteau
CREATIVE INTUITION IN ART AND POETRY
Wilder, A.
MODERN POETRY AND CHRISTIAN TRADITION
SPIRITUAL ASPECTS OF THE NEW POETRY
Watkin, W.
PLANNING THE MODERN CHURCH
*Tillich
Architectural Forum Dec. 1955 "Theology and the Modern Church"
Eastman
RELIGIOUS DRAMA
Bailey
THE ARTS AND RELIGION
6. Total analysis of the present situation in the light of preceding periods.
Berdyaev. N.
THE FAITH OF MAN IN THE MODERN WORLD
*Schweitzer, A.
THE DECAY AND RESTORATION OF CIVILIZATION
Sorokin, P.
CRISIS OF OUR AGE
Niebuhr, Reinhold
DOES CIVILIZATION NEED RELIGION?
*Spengler, O.
HE DECLINE OF THE WEST
Toynbee, A.
CIVILIZATION ON TRIAL
*Tillich, P.
THE RELIGIOUS SITUATION
Tillich, P.
"The World Situation" in THE CHRISTIAN ANSWER ed. Van Dusen
Jaspers, K.
MAN IN THE MODERN AGE
7. Continuation of the analysis of present day industrial society. Its relation
to religion.
Mannheim, K.
DIAGNOSIS OF OUR TIME
*Mumford, L.
TECHNICS AND CIVILIZATION
8. Religion and Education. The meaning of education (the wherefrom and the whereto)
9. The meaning and method of religious education. (Question and answer. Symbols
and interpretation.)
*Ulich
HISTORY OF EDUCATIONAL THOUGHT
*THE MEANING OF EDUCATION
CRISIS AND HOPE IN AMERICAN EDUCATION
*Sherill, L.
THE STRUGGLE OF THE SOUL
Smart, J.
THE TEACHING MINISTRY OF THE CHURCH
10. Religion and the University. (The possible function of a theological faculty)
*Moberly, Sir W.
CRISIS IN THE UNIVERSITY
Nash, A.
THE UNIVERSITY AND THE MODERN WORLD
RELIGIOUS PERSPECTIVES IN COLLEGE TEACHING
Jaspers, K.
THE IDEA OF THE UNIVERSITY
*Thomas, G.
RELIGION IN AN AGE OF SECULARISM
11. Religion and ethics: General (Formal ethics). The source of the moral "ought"
and the source of the content. The unconditional character and the question
of guilt.
12. Ethical relativism, love and kairos.
13. Ethics of being and ethics of law (ought to be) (law, grace and depth psychology,
demonic structure
*Tillich, P.
"Ethics in a Changing World" in THE PROTESTANT ERA
Ramsey, P.
BASIC CHRISTIAN ETHICS
*Bonhoeffer, D.
ETHICS
Niebuhr, R.
THE INTERPRETATION OF CHRISTIAN ETHICS
*Tillich, P.
LOVE, POWER AND JUSTICE

*Kant, I.
THE CRITIQUE OF PRACTICAL REASON
*Nietzsche
THE WILL TO POWER
Menninger, K.
LOVE AGAINST HATE
*Tillich, P.
THE COURAGE TO BE
14. The ethical subject: The idea and the ideal of personality. (The autonomous
and the theonomous personality)
*Jung, C. G.
MODERN MAN IN SEARCH OF A SOUL
May, R.
MAN'S SEARCH POR HIMSELF
*Fromm, E.
MAN FOR HISELF
*Buber, M.
I THOU
Tillich
"Psychotherapy and a Christian Interpretation of Human Nature"
in Review on Religion, March 1943.
15. Community and collective. We-consciousness, conformity, patternisation: The
non-personal character of the group (non-conformism).
*Ortega y Gasset
THE REVOLT OF THE MASSES
Mumford, L.
THE CULTURE OF CITIES
*Riesner
THE LONELY CROWD
Eliot, T. S.
THE IDEA OF A CHRISTIAN SOCIETY
Pope, L.
RELIGION AND CLASS STRUCTURE
*Schumpeter, I.
IMPERIALISM AND SOCIAL CLASSES
*World Council of Churches Symposium "Man's Disorder and God's Design" chapter
by Paul Tillich "The Disintegration of Society in Christian
Countries"
16. Types of authoritity and the meaning of religious authority.
*Schweitzer, A.
in CIVILIZATION AND ETHICS
Fromm, E.
ESCAPE FROM FREEDOM
*Barth, K.
THE WORD OF GOD AND THE WORD OF MAN
Berdyaev, N.
FREEDOM AND SLAVERY
*Loewe, A.
THE PRICE OF LIBERTY
17. Family and sex relations. (Historical relativity. The two meanings of sex)
Russell, G.
MEN AND WOMEN
*Cole
CHRISTIANITY AND SEX
Bailey, D. S.
MYSTERY OF LOVE AND MARRIAGE
HOMOSEXUALITY IN THE WESTERN CHRISTIAN TRADITION
Student World, no. 4 (1955) issue on "Christianity and sex"
18. The concept and function of "the economic." (Economic materialism)
*Tawney
CHRISTIANITY AND THE RISE OF CAPITALISM
*Heimann, E.
FREEDOM AND ORDER
HISTORY OF ECONOMIC DOCTRINES
Brunner, E.
JUSTICE AND SOCIAL ORDER
*Weber, M.
THE PROTESTANT ETHIC AND THE SPIRIT OF CAPITALISM
*Troeltsch, E.
THE SOCIAL TEACHINGS OF THE CHURCH
19. Religious Socialism. Basic ideas and limitations.
*Rauschenbusch, W.
THE THEOLOGY OF THE SOCIAL GOSPEL
Hutchison, J.
CHRISTIAN FAITH AND SOCIAL ACTION (1953) Symposium with articles
by P. Tillich, R. Niebuhr, J. Bennet
*Tillich, P.
THE PROTESTANT ERA
Kegley, P.
THE THEOLOGY OF PAUL TILLICH (chapter on Religious Socialism)
20. Justice, power and love (The realm of law)
*Tillich, P.
LOVE, POWER AND JUSTICE
Brunner, E.
"Love and Justice" in JUSTICE AND THE SOCIAL ORDER
*Nietzsche
THE WILL TO POWER OR AGAINST THE CHRISTIAN IDEA OF COMPASSION
*Tolstoy, L.
THE LAW OF LOVE AND THE LAW OF VIOLENCE
Haroutunian
LUST FOR POWER
Riordan, F.
THE CONCEPT OF LOVE IN FRENCH LITERARY REVIVAL
Montague, W.
SYMPOSIUM ON THE MEANING OF LOVE
Barnaby, J.
AMOR DEI

Humanities 127b (Theol. 185b)
Semester II, 1957-58
"Religion and Society"
Tues., Thurs. 11-12 (Sat.)
The assignments for this semester are:
a) 1600 pages of reading which include the starred selections below. (A bibli-
ography of all reading done for the course should be turned in with the
final examination. The books selected by the student should be annotated.)
b) A final examination dealing with the lectures and the starred selections in
the bibliography.
BIBLIOGRAPHY
Barth, Karl
Against the Stream
Bennett, John
Christian Ethics and Social Policy
Christianity and Communism
Bonhoeffer, D.
Ethics
Buber, Martin
*Between Man and Man (Parts I, III, IV)
Brunner, E.
*Christianity and Civilization (Vol. I & II)
Cole, W. G.
Sex in Christianity and Psychoanalysis
Fromm, Erich
*Man for Himself
Escape from Freedom
Jaspers, K.
The Idea of the University
Man in the Modern Age
Kegley, C. W. and
Bretall, R. W.
*The Theology of Paul Tillich (Chs. 1, 2, 3, 13, 14)
Kroner, Richard
Culture and Faith
May, Rollo
Man's Search for Himself
Miller, Alexander
*The Christian Significance of Karl Marx
Moberly, W. H.
Crisis in the Universit
Niebuhr, Reinhold
*Christian Realism and Political Problems
Ortega y Gasset
The Revolt of the Masses
Randall, John H.
The Making of the Modern Mind
Riesman, David
The Lonely Crowd
Sherill, Lewis
The Struggle of the Soul
Tawney, R. H.
Christianity and the Rise of Capitalism
Tillich, Paul
*The Protestant Era (Chs. II, IV, IX, XIII thru XVIII)
Tolstoy, L.
The Law of Love and the Law of Violence
Toynbee, A. J.
An Historian's Approach to Religion
Troeltsch, Ernst
*Christian Thought
Ulich, Robert
The Meaning of Education
Prof. Paul Tillich, Semitic Museum, Room 13. Office hours by appointment.
Mr. Robert Kimball, Assistant to Dr. Tillich. Office hours in Semitic 13 on
Tuesdays and Thursdays from 1:00 to 2:45.

FINAL EXAMINATTON
Humanities 127b
Theology 185b
Dr. Tillich
I. Explicate: evaluate, and concretely illustrate the follewing concepts:
"estrangement"
"Existential Hypothesis"
"Religious Socialism"
1¼ hours
II. Discuss the role of the "middle axioms" in Dr. Tillich's interpretation
of ethics, and relate your discussion to the problems of historical
relativism suggested by Troeltsch (in Christian Thought).
45 minutes
III. Explicate and evaluate Prof. Buber's understanding of "dialogue."
30 minutes
IV. Explicate and evaluate the significance of a discussion of "myths of
origin" for a contemporary understanding of political science.
30 minutes
Hand in the bibliography of the reading done for this course (including
annotations where pertinent) with the exam.

Humanities 127a
Professor Paul Tillich
1. Write the essay you have prepared on some topic of your choosing. (Be
sure to hand in the essay bibliography with your exam.)
(1 hr.)
2. Evaluate the following words or phrases, giving reference to the required
reading wherever pertinent:
– religion as 'ultimate concern'
– 'demythologizing'
– 'religious style, secular subject matter'
– 'non-objective space'
– "Christ against culture"
